title: Cyprus reunification
context: Cyprus has been divided since Türkiye's 1974 invasion, which followed a coup against President Makarios and left Turkish forces controlling roughly 37% of the island behind the UN-monitored Green Line, with some 200,000 people displaced. In 1983 the Turkish Cypriot leadership declared the Turkish Republic of Northern Cyprus, an entity UN Security Council Resolution 541 called "legally invalid" and which only Türkiye recognizes. Decades of UN-mediated efforts to build a bi-zonal, bi-communal federation have failed,
